summary: P0 crash fix verification: ChatPage.js `currentUser` → `user` rename (4 occurrences). Login → Dashboard → Project → ChatPage flow tested end-to-end. Zero TypeErrors, zero console errors. ChatPage renders correctly with messages and chat input.
passed_tests: Code review: ChatPage.js line 30 - `const { user } = useAuth()` confirmed (was currentUser)
Code review: ChatPage.js line 274 - `user?.departments?.length` confirmed (was currentUser)
Code review: ChatPage.js line 276 - `user.departments[0]` confirmed (was currentUser)
Code review: ChatPage.js line 715 - `currentUser={user}` prop passed to SourcePanel confirmed
Code review: AuthContext.js exports `user` (not currentUser) from context value
Frontend: Login with admin@ai.planetworkspace.com / Admin@123456 - succeeds, redirects to /dashboard
Frontend: Dashboard loads - project cards visible (data-testid='project-card-{id}')
Frontend: No TypeError on Project page
Frontend: ChatPage loads at /chats/{id} - data-testid='chat-page' found
Frontend: ChatPage has 6176 chars of content (not blank)
Frontend: Zero TypeErrors in console across all pages
Frontend: Zero console errors across all pages
seed_data_creation: None - used existing project (560694a9) and chat (7d34ab68) in excel project
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 fix verified. ChatPage.js uses `user` from useAuth() correctly. All 4 occurrences of currentUser renamed to user. App loads without any TypeError crash. Key test IDs: project-card-{id}, data-testid='chat-page', quick-chat-card-{id}. Auth: admin@ai.planetworkspace.com / Admin@123456.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: ChatPage.js was destructuring `currentUser` from useAuth() but AuthContext only exports `user`. This caused `currentUser` to be undefined, leading to TypeError when code attempted to read `.id` or `.departments` from it. Fix: renamed all 4 occurrences of `currentUser` to `user` in ChatPage.js. Verified fix resolves the crash with no regressions.
